{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/39668014?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/0xHunSec", "id": 39668014, "login": "0xHunSec", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM5NjY4MDE0", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/0xHunSec", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44307", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:N/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "## Summary

On Windows, a URI using backslash traversal (e.g. `\..\..\ secret.txt`) bypasses the directory traversal check in `Template.__init__` and the `posixpath`-based normalization in `TemplateLookup.get_template()`, allowing reads of files outside the configured template directory.


## Details

The root cause is a mismatch between `posixpath` (used for URI normalization in `get_template()`) and `os.path` (used for file access via `os.path.isfile()` and validation via `os.path.normpath()` in `Template.__init__`). On Windows, `os.path` is `ntpath`, which treats `\` as a path separator, while `posixpath` treats it as a literal character.

The vulnerability chain:

1. `get_template()` strips only leading `/` via `re.sub(r"^\/+", "", uri)` and normalizes with `posixpath` — backslash `\` is treated as a literal character, so `\..\ secret.txt` passes through with `..` undetected.
2. `Template.__init__()` validation uses `os.path.normpath()` — on Windows this resolves `\..\ secret.txt` to `\secret.txt`, which does not start with `..`, so the `startswith("..")` check passes.
3. `os.path.isfile()` on Windows interprets `\` as a path separator, resolving the `..` traversal and finding files outside the template directory.

### Affected code

- `mako/lookup.py`: `TemplateLookup.get_template()` uses `posixpath.normpath`/`posixpath.join` for path construction but `os.path.isfile()` for existence check
- `mako/template.py`: `Template.__init__()` URI validation uses `os.path.normpath()` which on Windows resolves backslash traversal to a form that passes the `startswith("..")` guard

## Impact

If an application passes user-controlled template names or include paths to `TemplateLookup.get_template()`, an attacker on Windows may be able to load and disclose readable files outside the configured template directory. The primary impact is local file disclosure. If the targeted file contains Mako/Python template syntax, it may also be parsed and executed as a template.

## Remediation

The fix should normalize backslashes to forward slashes early in the URI processing pipeline, before any path operations, to ensure consistent behavior across platforms.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00609, "percentile": 0.44521}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-06T21:45:16Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44307"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-12T22:16:37Z", "published_at": "2026-05-06T21:45:16Z", "references": ["https://github.com/sqlalchemy/mako/security/advisories/GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq", "https://github.com/sqlalchemy/mako/issues/435", "https://github.com/sqlalchemy/mako/commit/72e10c573ca0fbcbddd4455abca8ce92a61780d7", "https://github.com/sqlalchemy/mako/releases/tag/rel_1_3_12", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44307", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/sqlalchemy/mako/security-advisories/GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/sqlalchemy/mako", "summary": "Mako vulnerable to path traversal via backslash URI on Windows in TemplateLookup", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-13T16:43:11Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2h4p-vjrc-8xpq", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.3.12", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "Mako"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 1.3.11"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Mako vulnerable to path traversal via backslash URI on Windows in TemplateLookup
